target.title: fix(agents): strip reasoning tags from chat replies
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #89473.

- Treat `<reasoning>` and `<antml:reasoning>` as reasoning-control tags in the shared final assistant text sanitizer.
- Align the embedded stream/block-reply tag matcher and message recompute trigger so chat-channel streaming delivery strips those tags too.
- Add focused sanitizer, `extractAssistantText`, and existing table-driven subscriber regressions for regular and namespaced reasoning tags.

## Root Cause

The OpenAI-compatible stream partitioner already recognized `reasoning` tags, but the final sanitizer and embedded stream delivery matchers only covered think/thinking/thought variants. That left `<reasoning>...</reasoning>` text able to survive final assistant extraction or chat block-reply delivery.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Assistant-visible final text and embedded chat block-reply streaming now strip hidden `<reasoning>` and `<antml:reasoning>` blocks through the same reasoning-tag taxonomy used for existing think/thinking tags.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on this draft PR branch at `19877757f3`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec tsx --eval 'import { extractAssistantText } from "./src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.ts"; import { subscribeEmbeddedAgentSession } from "./src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.ts"; void (async () => { const visible = extractAssistantText({ role: "assistant", content: "Before <reasoning>secret</reasoning> After", timestamp: Date.now() } as any); const blockReplies:any[] = []; let handler: ((evt: unknown) => void) | undefined; const session:any = { subscribe(fn: (evt: unknown) => void) { handler = fn; return () => {}; } }; subscribeEmbeddedAgentSession({ session, runId: "proof", onBlockReply: (reply:any) => { blockReplies.push(reply); }, blockReplyBreak: "text_end", blockReplyChunking: { minChars: 5, maxChars: 50, breakPreference: "newline" } }); handler?.({ type: "message_start", message: { role: "assistant" } }); handler?.({ type: "message_update", message: { role: "assistant" }, assistantMessageEvent: { type: "text_delta", delta: "<reasoning>secret stream</reasoning>Visible reply" } }); handler?.({ type: "message_update", message: { role: "assistant" }, assistantMessageEvent: { type: "text_end" } }); await Promise.resolve(); await Promise.resolve(); const result = { extractAssistantText: visible, blockReplies }; console.log(JSON.stringify(result)); if (visible.includes("secret") || blockReplies.some((reply) => String(reply?.text ?? "").includes("secret"))) { throw new Error("reasoning leaked"); } })();'`

Evidence after fix: Direct proof printed `{"extractAssistantText":"Before  After","blockReplies":[{"text":"Visible reply","audioAsVoice":false,"replyToTag":false,"replyToCurrent":false}]}` and exited 0, so the hidden `secret` / `secret stream` reasoning payloads did not appear in extracted assistant text or chat block replies. Focused Vitest also passed 4 files / 200 tests across unit-fast and agents shards; `git diff --check` passed; `oxfmt --check` passed.

Observed result after fix: Closed regular and namespaced reasoning tags are stripped from visible assistant text and existing chat stream/block-reply reasoning-tag regressions now run for those tag names; unclosed reasoning-only content stays hidden instead of being recovered as visible text.

What was not tested: Full repository suite, broad changed checks, GitHub CI beyond the checks GitHub ran for this draft PR, and live MiniMax/LiteLLM reproduction.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec tsx --eval '...'` direct extraction + subscriber block-reply proof above
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.e2e-harness.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts`
target.author: monkeyleet
